SEARCH
Package neqsim.standards.gasquality

Class CriticalFlowOrifice

java.lang.Object
neqsim.standards.gasquality.CriticalFlowOrifice
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class CriticalFlowOrifice extends Object implements Serializable
Critical (choked / sonic) flow calculator for a gas flowing through an orifice or restriction. This is the basis for estimating the maximum (rupture) discharge rate through a fixed restriction such as a hole, broken instrument tapping or an orifice in a blowdown line, where the downstream pressure is low enough that the flow becomes sonic at the throat.

Choked flow occurs when the downstream-to-upstream pressure ratio falls to or below the critical pressure ratio:

rc = (2 / (k + 1))k/(k-1)

At and below this ratio the mass flow no longer increases with falling downstream pressure and is given by:

W = Cd · A · √(k · ρ1 · P1 · (2 / (k + 1))(k+1)/(k-1))

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • CriticalFlowOrifice

      public CriticalFlowOrifice()
      Default constructor for CriticalFlowOrifice.

  • Method Details

    • setGeometry

      public void setGeometry(double throatAreaM2, double cd)
      Sets the restriction geometry and discharge coefficient.
      Parameters:
      throatAreaM2 - throat (hole/orifice) area in m2 (must be > 0)
      cd - discharge coefficient (typically 0.62-0.85, must be > 0)

    • setUpstreamConditions

      public void setUpstreamConditions(double upstreamPressurePa, double upstreamDensityKgM3, double isentropicExp)
      Sets the upstream stagnation conditions.
      Parameters:
      upstreamPressurePa - upstream stagnation pressure in Pa absolute (must be > 0)
      upstreamDensityKgM3 - upstream density in kg/m3 (must be > 0)
      isentropicExp - isentropic exponent k = Cp/Cv (must be > 1)

    • criticalPressureRatio

      public static double criticalPressureRatio(double k)
      Computes the critical (choked) pressure ratio for the given isentropic exponent.
      Parameters:
      k - isentropic exponent (must be > 1)
      Returns:
      critical downstream-to-upstream pressure ratio (dimensionless)

    • isChoked

      public static boolean isChoked(double p1, double p2, double k)
      Returns whether the flow is choked for the given upstream and downstream pressures.
      Parameters:
      p1 - upstream pressure in Pa absolute (must be > 0)
      p2 - downstream pressure in Pa absolute (must be ≥ 0)
      k - isentropic exponent (must be > 1)
      Returns:
      true if the flow is sonic (choked)

    • calcCriticalFlow

      public void calcCriticalFlow()
      Runs the critical mass flow calculation. The flow is assumed choked (downstream pressure at or below the critical ratio); use isChoked(double, double, double) to confirm the regime for a specific downstream pressure.

    • getCriticalPressureRatio

      public double getCriticalPressureRatio()
      Returns the critical pressure ratio used in the calculation.
      Returns:
      critical pressure ratio (dimensionless)

    • getCriticalExpansionFactor

      public double getCriticalExpansionFactor()
      Returns the critical expansion factor.
      Returns:
      critical expansion factor (dimensionless)

    • getCriticalMassFlow

      public double getCriticalMassFlow()
      Returns the critical (choked) mass flow rate.
      Returns:
      critical mass flow rate in kg/s

    • isFlowChoked

      public boolean isFlowChoked()
      Returns whether the calculated condition is choked.
      Returns:
      true if the flow is choked

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes the calculation results to a pretty-printed JSON string.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of the results
